properties.title: Ukraine war briefing: EU sanctions oust Russian president of world chess
properties.summary: EU unveils largest package in four years of measures against Russian individuals and interests; Laura Loomer shows up in Kyiv. What we know on day 1,612 Arkady Dvorkovich, the president of the International Chess Federation (FIDE) , said on Friday he ⁠was suspending his activity with the organisation after being placed on the EU list of sanctioned Russians . Dvorkovich, a former Russian deputy prime minister, complained that the designation was “unlawful and unfair”. A statement said Viswanathan

properties.title: French PM’s office meets banks to try resolve far right’s financing problem
properties.summary: PARIS — Officials working for French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu have met with banking executives to explore ways to ease financing issues faced by the National Rally and other parties ahead of next year’s presidential election. An aide from the prime minister’s office, granted anonymity to discuss sensitive issues, told reporters that officials discussed ways to help politicians get bank loans to finance their presidential campaigns. One option on the table is a loan agreed between several

properties.title: Beethoven versus birds: ECB shortlists designs for new euro banknotes
properties.summary: BRUSSELS — Who would win in a fight between Ludwig van Beethoven and a kingfisher? We’re about to find out. The European Central Bank today unveiled its shortlisted designs for the first revamp of the euro banknotes since they were released in 2002. The public can weigh in on whether it wants to see European historical figures, or rivers and birds, on the currency. The first set of potential banknotes features American-Greek opera singer Maria Callas, German composer Beethoven, French-Polish sci

properties.title: Pride organisers vow to march in Romanian city that has blocked event four years in a row
properties.summary: Oradea is only major city in EU where local authorities have repeatedly blocked Pride plans Campaigners in a Romanian city have vowed to go ahead with a Pride march after local authorities denied permission for the fourth consecutive year. Since 2023, campaigners in the western city of Oradea, which has a population of about 180,000, have sought permission to hold Pride events. However, each year they have faced opposition, making Oradea the only major city in the EU where local authorities have

properties.title: Greek gas concession unlocks EU’s Russia sanctions package
properties.summary: BRUSSELS — EU countries reached a deal Thursday on a sweeping 21st sanctions package against Moscow, but only by allowing Greece to keep sending Russian gas to countries outside the bloc. The final sticking point — a proposed ban on shipping Russian gas outside the EU — was resolved through a limited exemption for contracts signed before Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, while prohibiting EU operators from entering into new ones, two EU diplomats said, speaking anonymously to discuss the

properties.title: Canada pushes new UK government to join defense bank
properties.summary: LONDON — Canada has urged the new-look British government to join its defense bank, as it tries to deepen its European presence. In an interview with POLITICO, Canadian Minister of National Defence David McGuinty said he is “hopeful” Britain will now sign up to Ottawa’s Defence, Security and Resilience Bank after several of its backers were given key jobs under new Prime Minister Andy Burnham’s administration. McGuinty said Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney will raise the issue directly

properties.title: EU countries weigh fresh plan to salvage Russia sanctions package
properties.summary: BRUSSELS — EU ambassadors will on Thursday examine a fresh proposal on a sweeping new set of Russia sanctions after Greece again vetoed the measures over concerts for its fuel shipping industry.  The fresh proposal, put forward by EU rotating presidency holders Ireland, offers a credible way out of the deadlock that has held up progress on the package for weeks, two diplomats familiar with the negotiations told POLITICO. They were granted anonymity to discuss the confidential discussions. “

properties.title: Post-Orbán sanctions showdown exposes EU capitals’ red lines
properties.summary: BRUSSELS — EU ambassadors will make another push Wednesday to finalize the bloc’s first Russia sanctions package since Viktor Orbán left office, after governments stripped back key measures to protect their energy, fish processing, banking and shipping interests. For years, Orbán — Hungary’s veteran populist leader who cultivated close ties with the Kremlin and repeatedly threatened to block sanctions — gave other capitals cover to resist tougher action.
